free_text: Charged particle-producing reactions of 15-MeV neutrons
on 51V and 93Nb
Rotating-target neutron source, 3x10**12 n/sec into
4pi
Magnetic quadrupole doublet lens and
associated reaction chamber, transport tube and
two-detector telescope with 2 silicon surface barrier
detectors: 15-microm thick delta-E and 1500-microm
thick E detector. Low cutoff energy 1.1 MeV for
protons, 1.8 MeV for deuterons, and 4.3 MeV for
alphas.
Spectrometer acceptance inferred from
measurements of spectra from CH2 and CD2 targets.
This calibration was made for each quadrupole
gradient setting.
Energy loss in target by outgoing charged particle
was corrected for by assuming that all particles
were created in center of target.
Errors include contributions from target
thickness, statistics, and neutron elastic scattering
cross sections from protons and deuterons used for
flux determination.
Approved by S.M.Grimes, 78/7/17.
